summary: Surah An-Najm affirms the truthfulness and divine inspiration of Prophet Muhammad, particularly referencing the Ascension (Mi'raj) and his vision of the supreme signs. It refutes pagan beliefs about idols and angels, emphasizing that guidance comes only from Allah. The surah stresses individual responsibility, that every soul will be judged for its own deeds, and recounts the destruction of previous peoples who denied the truth, warning of the approaching Day of Judgment.

groups.title: Section: By the Star: the Messenger speaks only revelation; the taught-by-the-Mighty vision at the Highest Horizon
